summary:
The foramen caecum is an anatomical feature located at the base of the tongue, arising from embryological remnants of the first branchial arch. It is not associated with the ethmoid or sphenoid bones; thus, it does not appear in those craniofacial structures.  

(Note: Only include content directly related to the query.)
